(sha=37d1567f84d63e1196bcf2fe8d18c85502a3295a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR strips plaintext provider `apiKey` values from config-derived generated `models.json` entries while preserving existing merge-mode plaintext keys and non-secret markers, with focused plan tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: an existing merge-mode provider with a non-marker `apiKey` flows through `mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets` and is written back into the final provider catalog. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the maintainer-owned security and compatibility policy for existing `models.json`-only credentials, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values from generated `models.json` provider entries before merge-mode preservation
- preserve existing `models.json`-only plaintext keys in merge mode so upgrades do not break users whose persisted file is their only credential source
- preserve existing non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers
- add plan-level regression coverage for plaintext stripping, marker preservation, and the `models.json`-only upgrade path

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Generated `models.json` should not persist a plaintext provider `apiKey` from source config while the provider-authenticated model path still works.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw build from this PR branch at `37d1567`, macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, temporary OpenAI-compatible server at `http://127.0.0.1:18081/v1`. The temporary server required a bearer token and returned `OK` from `/v1/chat/completions`. The source config contained a plaintext provider key; all proof output below is redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-84581/state4 node --input-type=module -e "import { ensureOpenClawModelsJson } from './dist/agents/models-config.js'; await ensureOpenClawModelsJson({ configPath: '/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-84581/openclaw.json' });"

FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-84581/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-84581/state4 OPENCLAW_AGENT_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-84581/state4/agent node openclaw.mjs models status --probe --probe-provider proof-openai-compat --probe-timeout 8000 --probe-max-tokens 4 --json
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

Generated `models.json` excerpt after running built OpenClaw runtime code:

```json
{
  "providers": {
    "proof-openai-compat": {
      "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:18081/v1",
      "api": "openai-completions",
      "models": [
        {
          "id": "proof-model",
          "name": "Proof Model",
          "reasoning": false,
          "input": ["text"],
          "contextWindow": 8192,
          "contextTokens": 8192,
          "maxTokens": 64
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

The generated provider has no `apiKey` field.

Provider probe output excerpt:

```json
{
  "defaultModel": "proof-openai-compat/proof-model",
  "resolvedDefault": "proof-openai-compat/proof-model",
  "auth": {
    "probes": {
      "totalTargets": 1,
      "results": [
        {
          "provider": "proof-openai-compat",
          "model": "proof-openai-compat/proof-model",
          "mode": "api_key",
          "status": "ok"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The generated `models.json` provider entry omitted the plaintext `apiKey`, and `openclaw.mjs models status --probe` returned `status: "ok"` for `proof-openai-compat/proof-model`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not test a real paid third-party provider. The existing `models.json`-only plaintext credential migration/runtime-auth path is still unresolved in this revision and is tracked by the current review finding.

## Tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.core.json`

Fixes #11829
